strengths: Iterative character sketches effectively showcase your creative thinking process and how your artistic ideas evolved over time.
Side-by-side comparisons of dialogue box designs clearly demonstrate your coding skills and how you customized the GUI to complement your art style.
weaknesses: Your research activities are not explicitly mapped to specific success criteria, limiting the perceived impact of each source on the final product.
Descriptions of creative-thinking skills remain at a general level; specifying sub-skills (e.g., brainstorming, experimentation) would deepen the analysis.
There is little concrete evidence of how your ATL skills evolvedâsuch as iteration counts, feedback summaries or reflection on setbacks.
You do not link individual research or tutorial sessions directly to particular product enhancements, missing opportunities to show skill application in context.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You provide compelling examples of creative thinking and technical skill in your sketches and GUI work, demonstrating practical application of ATL skills. To strengthen this section, map each research activity to targeted success criteria, detail the sub-skills employed, and include concrete evidence of skill evolutionâsuch as iteration logs or feedback notesâto illustrate the effectiveness and growth of your ATL application.

strengths: Effectively connects personal interest in art and visual novels to clearly articulated learning goals, demonstrating strong motivation and self-awareness.
Provides a realistic rationale for selecting an existing story and setting a 25-hour completion target, showing sound scope control and feasibility.
Grounds success criteria in relevant research literature, reflecting a thoughtful and evidence-based approach.
Presents a detailed plan table with clear sequencing and progress notes, highlighting strong organizational skills.
weaknesses: Learning goals lack measurable criteria and timelines, making it difficult to gauge progress objectively.
The overall plan would benefit from specifying key resources (software, reference materials) and estimated hours per task to strengthen feasibility.
Several entries in the success criteria table are incomplete or truncated, reducing clarity on testing methods and expected outcomes.
No explicit criterion for narrative coherence (e.g., ensuring no plot holes), leaving a gap in evaluating story clarity and pacing.
Timeline omits contingency buffers for major tasks, risking delays if unexpected challenges arise.
A date entry is missing for the âAdd background noises and musicâ task, undermining consistency and trackability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning demonstrates clear personal motivation, realistic scope control and a strong organizational framework. By grounding success criteria in research and sequencing tasks effectively, youâve laid a solid foundation. However, to fully realize this plan you need to add measurable targets, complete and clarify all success-criteria entries, specify resources and time allocations, and build in contingency buffers. Addressing these gaps will make your plan more robust and trackable.

strengths: Offers a thoughtful discussion of challenges faced and their impact on your learning, reflecting strong self-awareness and depth in reflection.
Uses an evaluation table that directly links survey feedback to your success criteria, showing a clear connection between evidence and product assessment.
weaknesses: The reflection on future confidence is left incomplete, and there are no concrete goals for applying your new skills moving forward.
Your evaluation does not address every success criterionâFunction, Size and Pacing are only partially coveredâresulting in an uneven assessment.
Targeted growth insights are missing for areas like dialogue-art layout optimization and download-size refinement.
Lacks detailed, forward-looking action plans that build on identified strengths and address weaknesses.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflection demonstrates meaningful self-evaluation and uses survey data effectively to assess the product. To deepen this analysis, complete your thoughts on future confidence with specific goals, ensure every success criterion is thoroughly evaluated, and provide actionable insightsâsuch as strategies for improving layout or file optimizationâto guide your continued growth.

annotations.comment: Your research plan is comprehensive; strengthen it by mapping each source directly to one or more success criteria for targeted skill application.
The sentence on future confidence is incomplete. Finish this reflection and set concrete goals for applying your new skills moving forward.

Your learning goals articulate skill areas but lack measurable criteria. Define specific targets (e.g., number of background drawings or coding exercises) and timelines to gauge your progress clearly.
You effectively connect your personal interest in art and visual novels to your learning goals, demonstrating strong motivation and clear selfâawareness.
Your product goal is well-justified for feasibility, but the plan would benefit from specified resources and a brief timeline to ensure clarity on how you will achieve it.
Strong rationale for selecting an existing story and setting a 25-hour completion target shows realistic planning and scope control.
The success criteria table is insightful but some entries are incomplete. Ensure each criterion has clearly defined Reason and Testing Method to maintain consistency.
Your success criteria are grounded in research literature, demonstrating a thoughtful approach to defining what makes a strong visual novel.
The continuation of the first criterion is truncated. Review table content to ensure each description and testing method is fully captured and clear.
Consider adding an explicit criterion for narrative coherence (e.g., no plot holes) to ensure your pacing success criteria cover story clarity.
Your timeline is detailed, but specifying key resources (software, reference materials) and estimated hours per task will strengthen feasibility and sequencing.
The task on January 11 lacks a date entry for the âAdd background noises and musicâ step. Adding exact dates maintains consistency and helps you track progress.
Build in contingency buffers for each major task to accommodate unexpected delays and maintain steady progress towards your deadlines.
When describing Creative Thinking, specify which sub-skills (e.g., brainstorming, experimentation) were most critical to improving your designs for clarity and style.
Strengthen your reflection on ATL skill application by providing evidenceâsuch as iteration counts or feedback summariesâthat shows how your skills evolved.
Including iterative character sketches effectively demonstrates your creative thinking process and evolution of artistic ideas.
Your plan table shows clear sequencing and progress notes, reflecting strong organizational skills in achieving your product.
The side-by-side dialogue box comparison clearly evidences your coding skills in customizing the GUI to complement your art style.
Link each research activity to a specific product enhancementâfor example, cite how a tutorial influenced a particular coding feature or art decision.
You offer a thoughtful discussion of challenges and learning impacts, demonstrating strong self-awareness and depth in reflection.
Your evaluation table links survey feedback directly to success criteria, showcasing a clear connection between evidence and product assessment.
Extend your evaluation to cover each success criterion comprehensively, including areas for growth in Function, Size and Pacing for balanced reflection.
In your Function, Size and Pacing evaluation, add targeted growth insightsâsuch as how to optimize dialogue-art layout or refine download size beyond survey results.
